title: Bread Ahead Doughnuts
method: Ring all of dough ingredients apart from butter together into a bowl, tip onto the table, and knead for 8 mins
Continue to knewd the dough, adding the butter 1/4 at a time until incorporated
Knead the dough for a further 5 mins until glossy, smooth and very elastic when pulled
Return dough to a bowl, cover with a plate and leave to prove until doubled in size. Once doubled, knowck back the dough, then re-cover the bowl and put into fridge to chill overnight
The next day, take dough out of fridge and divide into 6 pieces

Roll dough into smooth tight buns and place on a floured / oiled baking tray, leaving plenty of room between them so they don't stick owhile proving. Cover lightly with oiled cling film or a large tray and leave for ~2 hours or until doubled in size
Prepare deep-fat fryer or fill a heavy-based saucepan with oil. Heat oil to 180C
Once oil is heated, remove doughnuts from the tray by sliding a floured pastry scraper underneath them, and transfer to hot oil
Fry for 2 mins on each side until golden brown - they'll puff up and float. Remove from the fryer and place on baking paper, then toss in a bowl of caster sugar while still warm
Repeat until all are fried - making sure to check oil temperature every time before frying
To fill the doughnuts, use a butter knife to make a hole in the crease of each one. Fill a piping bag with filling and pipe into doughnut
notes: Recipe taken following a doughnut workshop at breadahead bakery
2 day process because of chilling overnight before frying
Don't overcrowd the fryer - only fry 2 to 3 doughnuts at once
Oil temperature must be 180C. Too high = burnt and raw inside, Too low - doughnut absorbs oil and becomes greasy
The dough can alternatively be baked as brioche - oil a 2lb loaf tin, transfer dough from fridge and cover in tin to prove at room temerature for 2 hours. Bake at 180C for 25 mins.
